message:
The other day, as the sky turned a little darker and the wind started to pick up, I sat down with my phone and opened our family group chat. You know, the one that’s usually filled with memes and dinner plans? Well, suddenly it was buzzing with questions about storm supplies and power outages. My sister was asking if we had enough water, while my brother was looking for the flashlights he swore he had stored somewhere. 

It was a funny moment, really—this chaotic little thread of love and concern reminding us all to check in with each other, even in the midst of a storm. We ended up sharing tips on filling bathtubs with water (who knew?) and making sure our phones were charged. 

So, even when the weather gets wild, it’s nice to have that little reminder of family support. And hey, this little refresher helped us feel a bit more prepared! How do you and your family stay connected during storm season?
